message:
fix(sync): defer open-time JSONL recovery on --rename-prefix so the rename actually applies instead of being refused

Evolves the prior commit's approach. That commit detected the
"open-time recovery already rebuilt the DB, so --rename-prefix was
silently dropped" case and returned VALIDATION_FAILED with an
actionable re-run command. Useful, but it still forced the user to
run the same command twice — once to discover the conflict, once to
actually do the work.

This commit fixes the root cause: when the caller asks for
--rename-prefix, the open-storage path now DEFERS the implicit JSONL
recovery and hands back a freshly-opened empty SQLite file, so the
explicit import path that follows can apply the rename semantics
against the JSONL directly — one invocation, correct result.

src/config/mod.rs
-----------------
- New `JsonlRecoveryStrategy { RebuildFromJsonl, DeferToExplicitImport }`
  enum. Existing `open_sqlite_storage_with_recovery` now delegates to
  `open_sqlite_storage_with_recovery_strategy(..., RebuildFromJsonl)`
  so all existing call sites are byte-identical.
- New sibling `open_sqlite_storage_with_deferred_jsonl_recovery` uses
  the `DeferToExplicitImport` strategy: when the DB file is missing
  and the JSONL exists, it opens a fresh empty SQLite instead of
  rebuilding, logs a warn that the explicit import will populate it,
  and returns `(storage, false)` (auto_rebuilt=false).
- The DB-present-but-corrupt branch gets a matching
  `prepare_fresh_storage` closure that runs `rebuild_database_family_
  with_backup` under the deferred strategy, leaving the caller with
  a fresh DB but preserving a backup of the old one under
  recovery_dir (same guarantee the existing path gives).
- Public entrypoint `config::open_storage_with_cli_deferred_jsonl_
  recovery` wires the deferred strategy through the same CLI-
  override plumbing `open_storage_with_cli` uses. Import-only
  callers get to pick the strategy without each having to reimplement
  the overlay stack.

src/cli/commands/sync.rs
------------------------
- `execute()` now computes `defer_jsonl_recovery = !status &&
  !flush_only && !merge && rename_prefix` and routes the storage
  open through `open_storage_with_cli_deferred_jsonl_recovery` when
  true. That one condition is the entire behavior switch.
- The post-open `rename_semantics_were_skipped` detector now covers
  both "prefix mismatch" and "duplicate external refs" — the latter
  is the second case where the rename-prefix import path runs
  cleanup logic the implicit rebuild does not. Adds
  `jsonl_contains_duplicate_external_refs()` helper and exercises
  it in the detector expression.
- The VALIDATION_FAILED branch the prior commit added still fires,
  but only for the narrow residual case where the deferred strategy
  cannot be used (e.g., `--status` was also set alongside
  `--rename-prefix`) and recovery happened anyway. For the common
  path — just `br sync --import-only --rename-prefix --force` against
  a missing DB — no error, the import just works.

tests/e2e_errors.rs
-------------------
- Rewrites `e2e_sync_auto_rebuild_rejects_rename_prefix_after_recovery`
  into `e2e_sync_rename_prefix_applies_after_missing_db_recovery_
  with_force`: same fixture setup (prefix-mismatched JSONL, missing
  alternate DB), but now the assertion is that the command SUCCEEDS
  (exit 0, `created=1` in the JSON payload), the alternate DB
  contains exactly one row, and that row's id starts with the
  configured `target-` prefix. The "must re-run" error message is
  no longer emitted on this path.
- Adds `test_jsonl_contains_duplicate_external_refs_detects_
  duplicates` unit test exercising the new helper: fixtures with and
  without duplicate external_refs, and with a tombstone to confirm
  the helper ignores tombstones the same way the prefix-mismatch
  helper does.
- Keeps existing coverage for plain `--rebuild` (no rename), plain
  `--force` (no rename), and missing-DB happy path intact.
